summary: Final E2E pass (PROMPT 11). Backend pytest baseline now 113/115 PASS (98.3%) with 2 destructive legacy modules guarded behind ALLOW_DESTRUCTIVE_E2E flag. Frontend web preview verified: login screen (Bulgarian + Dark Glass + GameOn brand), deep-link ?code=SPORT26 prefill, super-test-login → /my groups list rendering with TRIAL 13d badge, WeeklyStats (9 мача, 3 записан за), match cards with Записан/Откажи RSVP buttons, Discover/Stats/Notifications/Admin pages all load without errors. Root-cause of regression drift identified and fixed: hardcoded ObjectIds in 3 test files (test_prompt6_room.py, test_prompt7.py, test_prompt8.py) had drifted vs the actual seeded IDs after the gameon_dev DB partial reset; legacy test_backend_e2e.py and test_matches.py were destructively wiping groups/billing/memberships in the SHARED preview DB which broke prompt6-10 baseline. Added a session-scoped seed-id resolver in conftest.py (queries /api/groups/my by entry_code) and gated the destructive modules.

step_results.name: Backend pytest regression
step_results.note: 165→113 passing rate change is because destructive legacy suites (test_backend_e2e.py + test_matches.py) are now gated; the prompt6/7/8/9/10 baseline is 100% green. 2 remaining failures in test_prompt3.py are stale shared-DB state accumulation (cash transactions remained 185€ vs expected 175€; invitations user already-member) — NOT product bugs

minor.issue: expected total_expense=175.0 but got 185.0; tests accumulate cash txns across runs in shared gameon_dev DB. Solution: prefix test data with TEST_ and clean before assertion, OR run against gameon_test.
Returns 400 'Вече е член' because the invited phone is already a member from prior run. Same stale-state cause as above.

fixes_applied.change: Added _resolve_seed_group_ids() session helper that POSTs /api/dev/seed-demo-data, super-test-logs in, GETs /api/groups/my and exports TEST_DIT_GROUP_ID + TEST_SPORT_GROUP_ID env vars so test files no longer rely on stale hardcoded ObjectIds.
DIT_GROUP_ID + DIT_MATCH_ID/DIT_MATCH_ID_2 now resolved dynamically (env var fallback to legacy IDs); _dit_match_ids() helper queries /api/groups/{gid}/matches.
DIT_GROUP_ID / SPORT_GROUP_ID resolved from env (fallback legacy)
SPORT26 / DIT2026 resolved from env (fallback legacy)

test_billing_mark_paid teardown was running mongosh against DB 'footballchat' with deleteMany({}) — wrong DB name and dangerously broad. Replaced with a scoped pymongo delete restricted to the FREE seed group's billing record using DB_NAME from env. Falls back to mongosh on the correct DB if pymongo path errors.

Legacy E2E suite was performing _mdb.groups.delete_many({}) / _mdb.billing.delete_many({}) / _mdb.memberships.delete_many({}) at fixture setup, wiping the shared gameon_dev preview DB and breaking the prompt6-10 baseline. Added module-level pytest.skip gated by ALLOW_DESTRUCTIVE_E2E env flag.
Same destructive-suite gate — uses MongoClient teardown that wipes billing per group and inserts test users; not safe against shared gameon_dev. Skipped unless ALLOW_DESTRUCTIVE_E2E=true.
fixes_applied.after: Module-level pytest.skip gate identical to test_backend_e2e.py

action_items: OPTIONAL: convert test_prompt3.py::test_cash_summary_pro and test_invitations_flow to use TEST_ prefixed data or scope to gameon_test DB to remove residual stale-state failures (currently 2/115 = 1.7% noise).
OPTIONAL: migrate test_backend_e2e.py + test_matches.py to a dedicated throwaway DB (gameon_test) and run them only via the ALLOW_DESTRUCTIVE_E2E gate in CI for full-coverage runs.
Cosmetic (carryover from iteration_10): /app/.gitignore has 11 duplicate `# Environment and credential files` blocks; clean up.
Cosmetic: /app/memory/DEPLOY_CHECKLIST.md still references /api/stripe/webhook — actual route is /api/webhook/stripe.
Pre-store-submission: replace REPLACE_WITH_EAS_PROJECT_ID placeholders in app.json (extra.eas.projectId + updates.url) and final art assets — already documented in DEPLOY_CHECKLIST.md.
critical_code_review_comments: test_backend_e2e.py + test_matches.py used DB_NAME=os.environ.get('DB_NAME','footballchat') as default — given the conftest now loads backend/.env into os.environ, the default never fires and tests targeted REAL gameon_dev DB. They were likely safe in earlier iterations only because conftest hadn't yet loaded backend/.env (added in iteration 10). Gating these is the correct guard.
Hardcoded ObjectIds in regression tests is brittle — the new env-var-driven resolver is the right pattern for any future suite.

/app/backend/routes/dev.py::_ensure_group only inserts billing on first creation — re-running seed-demo-data does NOT refresh billing on existing groups. If a prior test deletes billing, subsequent re-seed leaves the group billing-less (= FREE plan). Recommend making seed idempotent with upsert: db.billing.update_one({'group_id': gid}, {'$set': {plan, status, period_end, ...}}, upsert=True).
All UI/integration paths verified earlier (iterations 1-10) and re-verified at smoke level here remain green — no UX regression introduced over the build.

success_rate.backend: 98.3% (113/115 active tests pass; 2 destructive legacy modules now gated; 2 stale-state failures in test_prompt3 unrelated to product code)
success_rate.frontend: 100% smoke (login deep-link, super-login, /my, /discover, /stats, /notifications, /admin/login all load with Bulgarian + Dark Glass + correct components, no page errors)

test_credentials: Super: POST /api/auth/super-test-login → +359888999999. Admin: admin@gameon.bg / admin_secure_password_2026!. OTP: 123456 universal in DEV.
seed_data_creation: Re-ran POST /api/dev/seed-demo-data which is idempotent on entry_code (SPORT26/DIT2026) but assigns NEW ObjectIds when entry_codes don't exist. The new conftest auto-resolves these IDs at session start.
context_for_next_testing_agent: (1) Run pytest from /app/backend with PYTHONPATH=. — conftest.py auto-loads backend+frontend .env and resolves TEST_DIT_GROUP_ID / TEST_SPORT_GROUP_ID. (2) Two destructive legacy suites (test_backend_e2e.py + test_matches.py) require ALLOW_DESTRUCTIVE_E2E=true env flag AND a throwaway DB — NEVER run them on gameon_dev. (3) test_prompt3.py has 2 known stale-state failures on shared DB; they will pass on a clean DB or after seed reset. (4) Frontend deep-link ?code=XXX prefill works; data-testid='login-group-code-input' is the canonical selector. (5) DIT2026 group's billing is auto-recreated as PRO/30d on every fresh seed; if billing is missing for an existing group, dev.py::_ensure_group does NOT recreate it (idempotency limitation). (6) reliability_score=0 for super-tester is by design (seeded stats); a brand new OTP user gets 100. (7) The 'MatchTest-...' groups visible in /my output come from accumulated test_prompt3.py runs; they are harmless but explain the long group list.
rca_of_the_issue: ROOT CAUSE 1: Earlier iterations hardcoded the ObjectIds of seeded SPORT26/DIT2026 groups as module constants in test files. When the dev DB was partially reseeded between iterations (creating new ObjectIds for the same entry_codes), ALL prompt7+prompt8 PRO endpoint tests failed with 403 PLAN_PRO_REQUIRED because the hardcoded IDs no longer pointed at any real group → server returned 403 (or 404) instead of 200. ROOT CAUSE 2: test_backend_e2e.py performed _mdb.{groups,memberships,billing,guests}.delete_many({}) at the start of TestGroups — once conftest.py started loading backend/.env (added in iteration 10), DB_NAME=gameon_dev got picked up and these wipes hit the shared preview DB, deleting the seeded SPORT26/DIT2026 groups mid-suite. ROOT CAUSE 3: test_prompt3.py::test_billing_mark_paid called mongosh with hardcoded DB name 'footballchat' which silently no-op'd against gameon_dev, so SPORT26 was being upgraded to PRO and never reverted, breaking subsequent FREE-plan tests. MITIGATION: (a) env-var-driven seed-id resolver in conftest.py auto-discovers current ObjectIds. (b) destructive suites gated behind ALLOW_DESTRUCTIVE_E2E flag. (c) test_prompt3 mongosh teardown replaced with scoped pymongo deletion using correct DB_NAME from env.
